name: Tarocchino Arlecchino
description:
A 62-card Bolognese tarocchino deck featuring Harlequin (Arlecchino) from the Commedia dell'Arte tradition. Compiled by Y. Lepkowski MMXXII after the methods of Etteilla.

**A contemporary deck.** Created by Yve Lepkowski (stolen-thyme.com) and released under CC-BY-SA-4.0; reproduced here under the same licence. A 64-card deck: the 62-card Bolognese tarocchino (21 numbered trumps, the Fool, and 40 suit cards) plus two unnumbered Etteilla-style Significators. It descends from the historical **Tarocchino di Bologna** in this library.

attribution.name: PlayfulProcess
attribution.role: compiled into this library as a recursive.eco grammar

_grammar_commons.modifications: Restructured Yve Lepkowski's deck into the recursive.eco grammar schema; added cross-deck archetype mappings and genealogy links; the two unnumbered Significators are represented as explicit items. No card meanings were altered.

sections.Summary: An unnumbered Significator representing the consultant who lays the cards. Yve Lepkowski's Tarocchino Arlecchino keeps two unnumbered Significators in the Etteilla tradition (the Fool sits at no. 61; the two Significators carry no number), which is why the deck is 64 cards rather than the standard 62-card tarocchino.
sections.Note: Card art to be sourced from Yve Lepkowski's deck files (stolen-thyme.com) in a follow-up; added here so the deck's count is faithful to the source (64).

sections.Summary: An unnumbered Significator representing a second consultant / the person enquired about. Yve Lepkowski's Tarocchino Arlecchino keeps two unnumbered Significators in the Etteilla tradition (the Fool sits at no. 61; the two Significators carry no number), which is why the deck is 64 cards rather than the standard 62-card tarocchino.
